$comment: Source table for the starship-fittings compendium. Transcribed from reference/temp/swn-starship.txt (SWN Building a Starship, pp. 96-103). Encoding: cost/power/mass are BASE (fighter-class) values; costScales = PDF '*' (x10 frigate / x25 cruiser / x100 capital); powerScales/massScales = PDF '#' (x2 frigate / x3 cruiser / x4 capital, rounded up). specialCost = PDF 'Special' cost (cost stored as 0). Generated into packs/source/starship-fittings/ by import-scripts/generate-starship-fittings.mjs.

fittings.name: Advanced Lab
fittings.effect: Skill bonus for analysis and research.

fittings.name: Advanced Nav Computer
fittings.effect: Adds +2 for traveling familiar spike courses.

fittings.name: Amphibious Operation
fittings.effect: Includes the benefits of Atmospheric Configuration and allows the ship to operate while immersed in a liquid medium. Only fighter and frigate hulls can mount this fitting.

fittings.name: Armory
fittings.effect: Weapons and armor for the crew. There is enough gear to outfit the entire crew for normal use, but giving it away or losing it in use may deplete it.

fittings.name: Atmospheric Configuration
fittings.effect: Can land. Must be installed at build time; cannot be installed on cruiser-class or larger ships. Frigates and fighters only.

fittings.name: Auto-Targeting System
fittings.effect: Fires one weapon system without a gunner.

fittings.name: Automation Support
fittings.effect: Ship can use simple robots as crew.

fittings.name: Boarding Tubes
fittings.effect: Allows boarding of a hostile disabled ship.

fittings.name: Cargo Lighter
fittings.effect: Orbit-to-surface cargo shuttle. Only capable of surface-to-orbit flight (roughly 20 minutes either way); can latch onto a standard pressurized cargo container holding up to 200 tons. Can be purchased multiple times.

fittings.name: Cargo Space
fittings.effect: Free mass traded for pressurized cargo space: 2 tons in a fighter, 20 in a frigate, 200 in a cruiser, 2000 in a capital ship. Can be purchased multiple times.

fittings.name: Cold Sleep Pods
fittings.effect: Keeps a number of people equal to the ship's maximum crew in stasis indefinitely. Can be installed multiple times.

fittings.name: Colony Core
fittings.effect: Ship can be deconstructed into a colony base supporting up to five times its maximum crew. Once activated it cannot be re-packed.

fittings.name: Drill Course Regulator
fittings.effect: Common drill routes (no longer than twice the pilot's skill level, no course trimming) become auto-successes.

fittings.name: Drive-2 Upgrade
fittings.effect: Upgrade a spike drive to drive-2 rating.

fittings.name: Drive-3 Upgrade
fittings.effect: Upgrade a spike drive to drive-3 rating.

fittings.name: Drive-4 Upgrade
fittings.effect: Upgrade a spike drive to drive-4 rating.

fittings.name: Drive-5 Upgrade
fittings.effect: Upgrade a spike drive to drive-5 rating.

fittings.name: Drive-6 Upgrade
fittings.effect: Upgrade a spike drive to drive-6 rating. A captain needs only buy the final grade desired.

fittings.name: Drop Pod
fittings.effect: Stealthed, armored landing pod for troops. Twice as fast as a cargo lighter, applies -3 to tracking/targeting checks against it, and can carry up to 100 troops. Can be purchased multiple times.

fittings.name: Emissions Dampers
fittings.effect: Adds +2 to skill checks to avoid detection. Doubles all in-system travel times while engaged.

fittings.name: Exodus Bay
fittings.effect: Houses vast numbers of cold sleep passengers: up to 1,000 in a cruiser, 5,000 in a capital ship. Each further selection doubles capacity.

fittings.name: Extended Life Support
fittings.effect: Doubles maximum crew size. Can be fitted multiple times, each adding another 100% of the base maximum.

fittings.name: Extended Medbay
fittings.effect: Allows medical treatment of up to the ship's entire maximum crew at once, including critically wounded patients.

fittings.name: Extended Stores
fittings.effect: Maximum life support duration is doubled. Can be fitted multiple times.

fittings.name: Fuel Bunkers
fittings.effect: Adds fuel for one more drill between fuelings. Can be installed multiple times.

fittings.name: Fuel Scoops
fittings.effect: Ship can scoop fuel from a gas giant or star. Extraction takes four days but completely refuels the ship.

fittings.name: Hydroponic Production
fittings.effect: Ship produces life-support resources for a number of crew equal to its maximum crew. Can be taken multiple times, each additional selection doubling supported crew.

fittings.name: Lifeboats
fittings.effect: Single-use escape craft for the ship's maximum crew (up to 20 people per boat), with comms and basic survival supplies.

fittings.name: Luxury Cabins
fittings.effect: 10% of the ship's maximum crew get luxurious quarters.

fittings.name: Mobile Extractor
fittings.effect: Space mining and refinery fittings; can extract resources from asteroids and planetary surfaces. Operating one requires at least five crew.

fittings.name: Mobile Factory
fittings.effect: Self-sustaining TL4 fabrication plant and repair facilities; can create and repair vehicles, equipment, and structures. Operating one requires at least 100 well-trained personnel.

fittings.name: Precognitive Nav Chamber
fittings.effect: Allows a character with Precognition-2+ psionic skill to assist in interstellar drills.

fittings.name: Psionic Anchorpoint
fittings.effect: Focal point for allied psychics' powers; a rare pretech relic, unavailable on the open market in most sectors.

fittings.name: Sensor Mask
fittings.effect: At long distances, disguises the ship as another vessel of the captain's choice.

fittings.name: Ship Bay/Fighter
fittings.effect: Carrier housing for one fighter-class ship. Can be taken multiple times.

fittings.name: Ship Bay/Frigate
fittings.effect: Carrier housing for one frigate-class ship. Can be taken multiple times.

fittings.name: Ship's Locker
fittings.effect: General TL4 equipment for the crew; giving it away or losing it in use may deplete it until restocked.

fittings.name: Shiptender Mount
fittings.effect: Allows another ship (one hull size smaller) to hitch on the craft's spike drills for intersystem drills.

fittings.name: Smuggler's Hold
fittings.effect: Small amount of well-hidden cargo space that evades standard customs inspection.

fittings.name: Survey Sensor Array
fittings.effect: Improved planetary sensory array; any rolls with it add +2 to skill checks.

fittings.name: System Drive
fittings.effect: Replaces the drive-1 spike drive with a smaller system drive: the ship keeps drive-1 for maneuvering/system transit but cannot make interstellar drills. Lowers hull cost by 10% and adds free Power (1 fighter/2 frigate/3 cruiser/4 capital) and twice that amount of free Mass. This item's power value already represents that added free Power (accounting simplification); no separate mass bonus is auto-applied.

fittings.name: Teleportation Pads
fittings.effect: TL5 pretech teleportation to and from the ship: up to a dozen people or 1,200kg of matter, usable once every five minutes. Costs 200k where the tech is widely available.

fittings.name: Tractor Beams
fittings.effect: Manipulate objects in space at a distance, pushing/pulling/sliding objects up to one hull class smaller.

fittings.name: Vehicle Transport Fittings
fittings.effect: Halves the effective cargo tonnage of carried vehicles, including mechs and other military craft.

fittings.name: Workshop
fittings.effect: Automated TL4 tech workshops for maintenance and modification below a hull's maximum size.

defenses.name: Ablative Hull Compartments
defenses.effect: +1 AC, +20 maximum hit points, by sacrificing empty hull space to a complex system of ablative blast baffles.

defenses.name: Augmented Plating
defenses.effect: +2 AC, at the cost of speed and maneuverability. Can decrease a ship's Speed below 0, applied as a penalty to Pilot tests.

defenses.name: Boarding Countermeasures
defenses.effect: Makes enemy boarding more difficult: hardened bulkheads, reinforced hatches, and automated kill corridors deny entry to intruders lacking shipyard-grade or military breaching tools.

defenses.name: Burst ECM Generator
defenses.effect: Negate one successful hit; can only be used effectively once per engagement.

defenses.name: Foxer Drones
defenses.effect: +2 AC for one round when fired, Ammo 5; cheaply constructed, short-lived drones that confuse foes with energy emissions.

defenses.name: Grav Eddy Displacer
defenses.effect: 1 in 6 chance of any given attack missing entirely, by randomizing the ship's motion vectors in sympathy with metadimensional gravitic currents.

defenses.name: Hardened Polyceramic Overlay
defenses.effect: AP quality of attacking weapons reduced by 5, via a complex glazing process that hardens the ship's armor surface.

defenses.name: Planetary Defense Array
defenses.effect: Anti-impact and anti-nuke surface defenses: gravitic braker guns and an upgraded nuke snuffer field deflect meteor impacts and dampen nuclear fission over a hemisphere-sized area. Useless in ship-to-ship combat.

defenses.name: Point Defense Lasers
defenses.effect: +2 AC versus weapons that use ammo; too small to damage ships, these lasers detonate or melt incoming torpedoes, fractal charges, and other ammunition-based munitions.

weapons.name: Multifocal Laser
weapons.description: Twinned assay and penetration lasers modulate the frequency of this beam for remarkable armor penetration. Popular on fighters intended for frigate or cruiser engagement.

weapons.name: Reaper Battery
weapons.description: Stepped tapping of the spike drive power plant emits a torrent of charged particles. Little armor penetration, but can fry a small ship's power grid in a strike or two.

weapons.name: Fractal Impact Charge
weapons.description: A spray of penetrator sabots using fractal surfacing to increase impact. Favored for bomber-class fighter hulls.

weapons.name: Polyspectral MES Beam
weapons.description: A rare example of pretech weaponry; a fighter equipped with a PMB can scratch even a battleship's hull.

weapons.name: Sandthrower
weapons.description: Projecting a spray of tiny, dense particulate matter, sandthrowers are highly effective against lightly-armored fighters.

weapons.name: Flak Emitter Battery
weapons.description: A baseline frigate anti-fighter system, this battery fires waves of lasers or charged particles to knock down small craft.

weapons.name: Torpedo Launcher
weapons.description: Capable of damaging even a battleship, torpedoes are cumbersome, expensive, and often the core of a line frigate's armament.

weapons.name: Charged Particle Caster
weapons.description: A focalized upgrade to the reaper battery, the CPC has a much better armor penetration profile.

weapons.name: Plasma Beam
weapons.description: With superior targeting and a smaller energy drain than a CPC, a plasma beam sacrifices some armor penetration.

weapons.name: Mag Spike Array
weapons.description: A storm of magnetically-accelerated spike charges is almost guaranteed to eradicate any fighter-class craft it hits.

weapons.name: Nuclear Missiles
weapons.description: Useless in ship-to-ship combat or against any TL4 planet with working nuke snuffers, but can still erase an entire lostworlder city without such protection.

weapons.name: Spinal Beam Cannon
weapons.description: One of the first spinal-mount class weapons, the SBC briefly channels the full power of the ship into a charged beam. It lacks the power and penetration of the more advanced gravcannon, but also takes less power to mount.

weapons.name: Smart Cloud
weapons.description: A swarm of self-directed microdrones sweeps over the ship. Their integral beam weaponry is too small to damage larger ships, but they can wipe out an attacking fighter wave.

weapons.name: Gravcannon
weapons.description: Using much the same principles as man-portable grav weaponry, the gravcannon causes targets to fall apart in a welter of mutually-antagonistic gravitic fields.

weapons.name: Spike Inversion Projector
weapons.description: The SIP uses the ship's spike phasing as an offensive weapon, penetrating the target with a brief incursion of MES energies that largely ignore attempts to evade.

weapons.name: Vortex Tunnel Inductor
weapons.description: A capital-class model of the Spike Inversion Projector, a VTI is capable of incapacitating a cruiser in two hits. Its bulk limits its utility against fighter-class craft, however.

weapons.name: Mass Cannon
weapons.description: By firing projectiles almost as large as fighter-scale craft, the mass cannon inflicts tremendous damage on a target. Serious ammunition limitations hamper its wider-scale use.

weapons.name: Lightning Charge Mantle
weapons.description: Modulation of the ship's power core emits a cloak of MES lightning. Larger spike drive craft can shunt the energies away harmlessly, but fighter-class ships are almost invariably destroyed if hit.

weapons.name: Singularity Gun
weapons.description: One of the few surviving pretech weapons in anything resembling wide currency, this capital-class weapons system fires something mathematically related to a miniaturized black hole at a target.
